title: Mader's Conjecture (Conjecture 2)
statement_text: There exists a least integer $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^+}(TT_k)$ such that every digraph $D$ with $\delta^+(D) \geq \mathrm{mader}_{\delta^+}(TT_k)$ contains a subdivision of $TT_k$.
context_text: Mader proved that $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^+}(TT_4) = 3$, but even the existence of $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^+}(TT_5)$ is still open. This conjecture, posed in 1985, is the central motivating problem of the paper, which seeks to verify it for special classes of digraphs.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for all oriented paths (Corollary 20, with $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^+}(P)=|V(P)|-1$), for all in-arborescences (Theorem 23), and for the union of two directed paths from $x$ to $y$ and one from $y$ to $x$ (Theorem 24); also studies an analogue via the dichromatic number parameter.
notes: PDF source — math notation reconstructed from context.
paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

title: Conjecture 3
statement_text: There exists a least integer $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^0}(TT_k)$ such that every digraph $D$ with $\delta^0(D) \geq \mathrm{mader}_{\delta^0}(TT_k)$ contains a subdivision of $TT_k$.
context_text: The paper shows Conjecture 3 is equivalent to Mader's Conjecture 2: if transitive tournaments are $\delta^0$-maderian, then $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^+}(TT_k) \leq \mathrm{mader}_{\delta^0}(TT_{2k})$ for all $k$. It is stated separately to make this equivalence explicit.
paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

title: Conjecture 4
context_text: Oriented trees are known to be $\delta^0$-maderian with $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^0}(T) = |T|-1$ via a greedy procedure, but whether they are $\delta^+$-maderian is open. This is posed as a natural weaker step towards Mader's Conjecture 2. The paper provides evidence by proving in-arborescences are $\delta^+$-maderian (Theorem 23) and all oriented paths are $\delta^+$-maderian (Corollary 20).
paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

title: Bermond–Thomassen Conjecture (Conjecture 5)
statement_text: For every positive integer $k$, every digraph with minimum out-degree at least $2k-1$ contains $k$ disjoint directed cycles.
context_text: Alon showed the weaker bound of $64k$ suffices; the conjecture asks whether the optimal bound $2k-1$ holds. It is mentioned as a related result about $\delta^+$-maderian digraphs; Theorem 8 together with known Erdős–Pósa results implies that disjoint unions of directed cycles are $\delta^+$-maderian.
paper_contribution: Mentioned as context; no direct progress, but Theorem 8 implies $k$ disjoint copies of $\vec{C}_2$ are $\delta^+$-maderian using the Erdős–Pósa property of $\vec{C}_2$ (Reed et al.) and the Directed Grid Theorem.
paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

title: Alon's Partition Conjecture (Conjecture 6)
statement_text: There exists a function $h$ such that every digraph with minimum out-degree $h(k)$ has a partition $(V_1, V_2)$ such that, for $i = 1, 2$, $D\langle V_i\rangle$ has minimum out-degree $k$.
context_text: The existence of $h(2)$ remains open. If true, this conjecture implies Conjecture 7 that the disjoint union of two $\delta^+$-maderian digraphs is $\delta^+$-maderian.
paper_contribution: Mentioned as motivation for Conjecture 7; no direct progress made.
paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

title: Conjecture 7
statement_text: If $F_1$ and $F_2$ are $\delta^+$-maderian, then the disjoint union of $F_1$ and $F_2$ is also $\delta^+$-maderian.
context_text: Alon's Conjecture 6 would imply this. Partial positive answers follow from the Erdős–Pósa property: Theorem 8 shows that if $F$ is $\delta^+$-maderian and has the Erdős–Pósa property, then $k \times F$ is also $\delta^+$-maderian for all positive integers $k$.
paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

title: Burr's Conjecture (Conjecture 10)
statement_text: Every digraph with chromatic number $2k-2$ contains every oriented tree of order $k$ as a subdigraph.
context_text: The $\chi$-maderian digraphs are exactly the oriented forests (Theorem 9), but the precise value of $\mathrm{mader}_{\chi}$ is unknown. Addario-Berry et al.'s Conjecture 14 for oriented graphs implies Burr's conjecture for antidirected trees.
paper_contribution: Mentioned as motivation; Conjecture 11 is stated as a weaker consequence. No direct progress made.
paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

title: Conjecture 11
statement_text: If $T$ is an oriented tree of order $k$, then $\mathrm{mader}_{\chi}(T) \leq 2k-2$.
context_text: Stated as an interesting step towards Burr's Conjecture 10, of which it is a direct consequence. The $\chi$-maderian digraphs are exactly the oriented forests, but determining $\mathrm{mader}_{\chi}$ precisely for each oriented tree is still open.
paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

title: Problem 12
context_text: The paper proves in Section 3 that every digraph is $\vec{\chi}$-maderian. Since every digraph of order $n$ is a subdigraph of $\vec{K}_n$, bounding $\mathrm{mader}_{\vec{\chi}}(\vec{K}_n)$ controls all cases. The paper establishes $\mathrm{mader}_{\vec{\chi}}(\vec{K}_n) \leq 4^{n^2-2n+1}(n-1)+1$ (Corollary 36), but the exact value is unknown.
notes: PDF source — dichromatic number denoted $\vec{\chi}$; upper bound exponent reconstructed from context as $n^2-2n+1 = n(n-1)-n+1$ for $\vec{K}_n$ with $n(n-1)$ arcs and 1 component.
paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

title: Addario-Berry et al. Conjecture (Conjecture 14)
statement_text: Let $D$ be a digraph. If $|A(D)| > (k-2)|V(D)|$, then $D$ contains every antidirected tree of order $k$ as a subdigraph.
context_text: The bound $k-2$ is best possible. This conjecture for oriented digraphs implies Burr's Conjecture 10 for antidirected trees, and for symmetric digraphs it is equivalent to the Erdős–Sós conjecture. It characterizes $\bar{d}^+$-maderian digraphs (Theorem 13).
paper_contribution: Mentioned as context; no direct progress made.
paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

title: Erdős–Sós Conjecture (Conjecture 15)
statement_text: Let $G$ be a graph. If $|E(G)| > \frac{1}{2}(k-2)|V(G)|$, then $G$ contains every tree of order $k$.
context_text: Addario-Berry et al.'s Conjecture 14 restricted to symmetric digraphs is equivalent to the Erdős–Sós conjecture. Ajtai, Komlós, Simonovits and Szemerédi announced a proof for sufficiently large $k$ in the early 1990s; partial solutions appear in the literature.
paper_contribution: Mentioned as an equivalent special case of Conjecture 14 for symmetric digraphs; no direct progress made.
paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

title: Problem 16
context_text: Since $k$-connected graphs have minimum degree at least $k$, Mader's undirected theorem implies graphs with connectivity at least $g(k)$ contain a $K_k$ subdivision. The analogous question for strong connectivity $\kappa(D)$ and strong arc-connectivity $\kappa'(D)$ of digraphs is open. Thomassen's Conjecture 17 would imply a positive answer for $\kappa$-maderianness.
paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

title: Thomassen's Conjecture (Conjecture 17)
statement_text: There exists $f_1(k)$ such that if $\kappa(D) \geq f_1(k)$, and $x_1, \ldots, x_k$ and $y_1, \ldots, y_k$ are distinct vertices of $D$, then $D$ contains $k$ disjoint dipaths $P_1, \ldots, P_k$ such that $P_i$ goes from $x_i$ to $y_i$ for all $i \in [k]$.
context_text: This conjecture would imply that all digraphs are $\kappa$-maderian (Problem 16) and would also imply Lovász's Conjecture 18 about even directed cycles in highly strongly connected digraphs.
paper_contribution: Mentioned as a sufficient condition for Problem 16; no direct progress made.
paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

title: Lovász's Conjecture (Conjecture 18)
statement_text: There exists an integer $p$ such that every $p$-strongly connected digraph has an even directed cycle.
context_text: Stated as a consequence that would follow from Thomassen's Conjecture 17. It is mentioned as further motivation for studying highly strongly connected digraphs.
paper_contribution: Mentioned as a corollary of Thomassen's Conjecture 17; no direct progress made.
paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number
